result.vlm_summary:
<TRAJECTA_DATA_40fab35e>page_state:
Scrolled to a product marketing section on Apple Watch Series 11 page showing battery life claim.
task_relevant_visible_text:
Apple Watch Series 11
Overview
Why Apple Watch
Tech Specs
Buy
Up to 13 hours of battery life.
Always a positive.
selected_candidate:
13 hours of battery life
constraint_evidence:
- Navigate to Apple’s website: supported (apple.com URL/title visible)
- Latest Apple Watch model: supported (page title/URL show Apple Watch Series 11)
- Determine average battery life: supported (battery life claim visible)
- Exact battery life value visible: supported (“Up to 13 hours of battery life”)
- Average/typical battery life wording: not_visible (only “Up to” is shown, not an average)
- Price/rating/date/other qualifiers: not_visible
action_target:
Continue scrolling or inspect nearby specs to confirm whether this is the relevant battery-life figure for the task.
success_signals:
Visible battery-life number for the latest Apple Watch model found.
failure_signals:
If the task requires an average rather than a maximum, this “Up to 13 hours” may not satisfy it.
uncertainty:
The page shows a maximum battery-life claim, not an explicit average; may need a specs section or footnote for the exact metric.</TRAJECTA_DATA_40fab35e>

args.query: Apple product page task asks for average battery life of latest model; agent opens latest model page, sees only 'Up to' battery-life figures and exits without verifying an average/typical value

items.task: find: answer to 'Does it come with a warranty?'
items.expected_behavior: The agent should have used the visible Warranty & Support information and answered in line with it, rather than claiming the item does not come with a warranty.
items.actual_behavior: The agent answered that the item does not come with a warranty even though the page showed a Warranty & Support section with a manufacturer warranty note.

evidence.claim: The task was to answer whether the Ello Syndicate 20oz reusable glass water bottle comes with a warranty.
Screenshot 8 shows the Amazon product page scrolled to the Warranty & Support section, where warranty information is visible.

The visible text on screenshot 8 includes 'Warranty & Support' and 'Manufacturer’s warranty can be requested from customer service,' showing that warranty-related information was available on the page.
The agent nonetheless answered '[ANSWER] It doesn't come with a warranty.' at the final step, which conflicts with the warranty information visible on screenshot 8.

items.regression_rule: When a page visibly contains warranty/support information, the agent must not answer 'no warranty' unless the visible text explicitly says there is no warranty; if the warranty terms are vague, answer with the available warranty wording instead.

items.task: find: answer to 'What's the flight option with the latest departure time on the return journey?'
items.expected_behavior: The agent should have navigated to or identified the return flight options and selected the one with the latest departure time from that list.
items.actual_behavior: The agent identified the latest departure time from the "departing flights" (outbound) list instead of the "return journey" list. The agent also provided an incomplete answer.

evidence.claim: The agent failed to recognize that the current view is for "departing flights" (outbound), not the "return journey" requested by the user. The agent also cut off the message mid-sentence.
The screenshot shows "Top departing flights" for the outbound leg, not the return journey.

items.regression_rule: When searching for flight information, the agent must explicitly verify whether it is looking at the outbound or return leg of the journey as requested by the user.

items.task: navigate: Search for the lowest-priced round-trip flight from Los Angeles to Seoul, departing on April 5, 2026, and returning on April 15, 2026.
items.expected_behavior: The agent should have sorted the flight results by price and selected the lowest-priced round-trip flight for the specified dates.
items.actual_behavior: The agent performed a search for flights from Los Angeles to Seoul for the specified dates. While the search results page displayed various options, the agent did not select the lowest-priced flight, instead exiting while the results were sorted by "top flights." The task specifically required finding the lowest-priced flight.

evidence.claim: The page is currently sorted by 'top flights,' not 'price.'
The agent exited without selecting the lowest-priced flight, failing to fulfill the core requirement of the task.

items.regression_rule: When a task requires finding the 'lowest-priced' item, the agent must ensure the results are sorted by price and that the lowest-priced option is identified and selected. Failure to select the correct item based on the specified criteria constitutes a wrong result.

args.expected_behavior: After reaching the latest Apple Watch model page, the agent should have verified a battery-life figure that satisfies the task's wording: an average/typical battery life for the latest model, or otherwise navigated to specs/footnotes to confirm the appropriate metric before exiting.
args.actual_behavior: The agent navigated to Apple Watch Series 11 and scrolled until battery-life numbers were visible, but the inspected screenshots show only 'Up to 13 hours of battery life' and later 'Up to 24 hours' / 'Up to 38 hours in Low Power Mode.' It exited without confirming any explicit average or typical battery-life value.
args.regression_rule: When a task asks for a specific metric like average battery life, the agent must not stop after finding only 'up to' or alternate-mode figures; it should verify that the visible value matches the requested metric or continue to specs/footnotes until it does.

evidence.claim: The trajectory task is to navigate to Apple’s website and determine the average battery life of the latest Apple Watch model.
On the Apple Watch landing page, Apple Watch Series 11 is visible and marked New, supporting that the agent identified the latest model before drilling in.
On step 14, the Series 11 page visibly shows 'Up to 13 hours of battery life,' and the high-detail read marks the exact value as visible but says average/typical wording is not visible.

On the final inspected step, the Series 11 page shows 'Up to 24 hours' and 'Up to 38 hours in Low Power Mode'; the high-detail read again says battery-life information is visible but a single average value is not visible.
The agent then sent '[EXIT]' at step 20 instead of continuing to a specs section or otherwise verifying the requested average battery-life metric.

A validated prior eval case similarly treats stopping after finding only non-task-matching result-state information, without verifying the requested criterion, as a failure that should be guarded by an explicit verification rule.

suggested_followups.label: Check specs section
suggested_followups.message: Inspect whether the Apple Watch Series 11 tech specs page exposes a typical battery-life metric instead of only 'up to' values.

suggested_followups.label: Review final answer behavior
suggested_followups.message: Confirm whether the agent ever sent the user a battery-life answer before exiting, or only navigated and stopped.
